name: Timed Door
description: A door or gate opens after a trigger and closes again when a countdown, condition, or reset rule expires.
design_notes: Timed doors need a single authority for timer start, pause policy, door_state, grace windows, and checkpoint restoration. The player should know what started the door, how long it remains usable, and whether failure resets the room, the door, or only the timer.
design_purpose: Create route-planning pressure between a trigger and a destination.
Combine puzzle solving with execution without requiring permanent failure.
Encourage players to read the room layout and optimize movement.
player_fantasy: The player feels the satisfaction of triggering a gate and reaching it just in time.

parameters.name: door_id
parameters.description: Stable door identifier used by state, animation, and save data.

parameters.name: timer_start_trigger_id
parameters.description: Trigger that starts or restarts the door countdown.

parameters.name: open_window_ms
parameters.description: Countdown interval before the door closes or changes state.

parameters.name: timer_pause_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule that controls countdown behavior during pause, dialogue, loading, or cutscenes.

parameters.name: checkpoint_reset_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule applied after death, reload, or room reset while the timer is active.

parameters.name: close_grace_ms
parameters.description: Small tolerance before closing so near-threshold crossings do not feel unfair.

edge_cases: The player crosses the door threshold on the same tick that the close window expires.
The start trigger is pressed again while the door is opening, open, closing, or blocked.
A save occurs with the door open and only part of the countdown remaining.
Co-op players are on opposite sides when the door closes.
common_bugs: Door visuals close but collision remains open, or collision closes before animation communicates it.
Countdown UI rounds differently from authority state and shows one second remaining after closure.
Pause menu freezes the animation but not the underlying timer.
Repeated trigger events start multiple timers and close the door early.
balancing_notes: Timed doors should test route planning more than loading screens, camera confusion, or animation delay.
Grace windows and checkpoint placement reduce frustration without removing urgency.
If the door is required, failure recovery should be quick and obvious.
accessibility_notes: Show countdown state with text, audio cues, animation, and icon changes rather than sound alone.
Offer relaxed timers or alternate routes where strict execution is not the main challenge.

implementation_notes.unity: Store door_id, door_state, started_at tick, and open_window_ms in a timer component. Drive Animator and collider from committed door_state, and use one timer instance guarded by a state version.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a door node with explicit state and a timer service that respects timer_pause_policy_id. Save remaining interval or trigger state according to checkpoint_reset_policy_id rather than relying on Timer node internals.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use an Actor Component or Gameplay Ability task for timed state, with server authority if multiplayer players can trigger the door. Replicate door_state and remaining countdown to UMG, and resolve threshold crossings with close_grace_ms.
implementation_notes.web: Keep countdown in the simulation model using a monotonic tick and render UI from remaining milliseconds. Browser visibility changes should follow the pause policy instead of silently consuming the open window.

relationships.reason: Pressure plates commonly provide the activation event that starts a timed door window.
Timed doors are localized timed challenges with a door state, grace window, and reset policy.
Assist options can extend door windows or provide clearer countdowns without changing the puzzle structure.

example_games.title: Portal 2
example_games.note: Uses timed doors and switches to combine puzzle logic with traversal timing.

example_games.title: Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time
example_games.note: Uses timed gates as part of traversal challenge rooms.
